A TORNADO DAMAGED SEVERAL INDUSTRIAL BUILDINGS OF A FARM EQUIPMENT DEALER NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF LA HIGHWAY 3131 AND LA HIGHWAY 1. SEVERAL LARGE DOORS WERE BLOWN IN AND A LARGE PORTION OF ROOF WAS TORN OFF. IN ADDITION WINDOWS WERE BROKEN. DEBRIS WAS STEW ABOUT 1/4 MILE INTO A OPEN FIELD AND TREES. A NEARBY RAILROAD SAFETY CROSS ARM WAS BROKEN OR SHEARED OFF FROM ITS MOUNT. A CELL PHONE PICTURE WAS MADE BY EYE WITNESS AT A NEARBY UNDAMAGED BUSINESS. PATH LENGTH 1/4 MILE WIDTH 30 YARDS.

*** 1 FATAL *** NWS GROUND SURVEY INDICATES A WEAK TORNADO INTITIALLY TOUCHED DOWN NEAR LALLIE KEMP HOSPITAL TURNING AN UNOCCUPIED MOBILE HOME ON ITS SIDE...PEELED A PORTION OF THE ROOF OF A NEARBY BUILDING. SEVERAL AUTOMOBILES WERE SHIFTED AND THEIR WINDOWS WERE BLOWN OUT. ONE FATALITY OCCURRED WHEN A 51 YEAR OLD WOMAN AND WAS THROWN INTO A VEHICLE IN THE PARKING LOT. THE TORNADO CIRCULATION CONTINUED EASTWARD FOR ABOUT A MILE PRODUCING INTERMITTENT DAMAGE PRIMARILY TO TREES.

WEST OF HIGHWAY 51...TREES AND POWER LINES WERE BLOWN DOWN. EAST OF HIGHWAY 51...DAMAGE WAS REPORTED AT A HOSPITAL WHEN A SMALL PORTION OF THE ROOF WAS PEELED OFF...AN UNOCCUPIED MOBILE HOME WAS ROLLED OVER...AND 15 TO 20 AUTOMOBILES IN THE PARKING LOT WERE SHIFTED AND WINDOWS BROKEN.

SEVERAL LOCATIONS IN DIBERVILLE COMMUNITY HAD WIND DAMAGE. ROOF OF STORAGE BUILDING PEELED OFF AND A FEW PLATE GLASS WINDOWS BLOWN OUT. AUTO DEALERSHIP HAD SEVERAL GARGE DOORS BLOWN IN...METAL SIDING PEELED OFF...SHOWROOM FACADE BLOWN OFF...AND LARGE SIGN DAMAGED. WIND GUSTS ESTIMATED IN 75 TO 90 MPH RANGE.

*** 1 INJ *** A TORNADO DAMAGED 13 HOMES. SEVERAL LARGE HARDWOOD TREES SNAPPED IN HALF HITTING ROOFS AND CRUSHING A NEARBY CAR. ONE PERSON WAS INJURED WHEN HIT BY A FALLING PART OF RAFTER. THE TORNADO FOLLOWED AN INTERMITTENT 3.1 MILE PATH. PATH WIDTH WAS 20 YARDS. THIS WAS A JOINT DAMAGE SURVEY CONDUCTED WITH THE HELP OF JEFFERSON PARISH EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT.
